rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Leav To File Verified Second Amended Petition; Points And Authorities And Declaration With Exhibits.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 17, 2008 LINE 1. PETITIONER RHONDA COLVIN'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Verified Second Amended Petition IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEK TO AMEND IN CONFORMANCE WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1324 AND THE FILING OF A DECLARATION THAT COMPLIES WITH C.C.P. SECTION 473 AND EXPLAINS WHY THE AMENDMENT IS NECESSARY, WHEN THE FACTS GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM WERE DISCOVERED AND EXPLAINS THE REASON FOR ANY DELAY IN SEEKING TO AMEND. THE FILING OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS OF APRIL 11, 2008 AND FEBRUARY 20, 2008. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 17, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs are unable to prove that decedent was exposed to defendant's product that contained an asbestos component. Decedent worked with and took apart compressors manufactured by defendant and defendant has admitted that certain of its compressors contained asbestos components. Defendant's interrogatory 17 uses the phrase "original or replacement" and does not clarify the record as to whether plaintiff is contending the equipment worked on was original or replacement. The record also is silent on the age of any of the equipment at issue. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 for failure to shift the burden of proof. It is granted as to punitive damages based on plaintiff's devoid interrogatory responses on this issue. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 17, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that plaintiff is unable to prove its case of exposure to a Leslie product containing asbestos placed on the product by Leslie. Decedent's testimony related to the Ranger creates a reasonable inference that the Leslie product was original equipment and the record reflects evidence that Leslie valves contained asbestos. Defendant's interrogatory 17 by the use of the phrase "original or replacement" fails to require plaintiff delineate what evidence plaintiff has to prove either that decedent was exposed to original equipment or replacement equipment. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 for failure to shift the burden of proof. It is granted as to issues 3, 4 and 5. Plaintiffs' general responses fail to set forth facts establishing a basis for these claims and plaintiffs have not submitted evidence creating a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MAN-LI HUANG VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 17, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, ELIZABETH AVANT, KARMI OBERWAGER AND LIN VELASQUEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Hearing required on Defendants' motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff admits fact 69 that defendants have proffered a legitimate reason for plaintiff's termination. Plaintiff must present substantial evidence of pretext or pervasive conduct that created a hostile work environment. Plaintiff's counsel is directed to focus on the facts that meet this burden. Plaintiff also needs to address each of the reasons given for the termination and point to evidence that establishes pretext or a hostile workplace. This applies to causes of action 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6. The Court fails to see evidence that supports the harassment claim. It appears that plaintiff has abandoned the theory of retaliation set foth in the complaint and it does not appear that there is evidence sufficient to support this claim. The defamation claim appears to fail because there is no showing of malice. There does not appear to be a basis for the Unfair Competition Claim or for punitive damages. =(302/PJM/PB)
